Neural networks are highly flexible and can indeed use a mix of different activation functions (e.g., ReLU, sigmoid, tanh) across layers to model complex patterns. While they excel at non-linear relationships (eliminating A), they can handle non-numerical data (e.g., text via embeddings), require varied training methods (not just SGD), and are versatile beyond visual data (ruling out B, D, E, and J).
